summary: The primary function of HIV sentinel surveillance is to provide reliable estimates of disease incidence across defined population segments. This baseline measurement supports broader efforts such as identifying at-risk communities, assessing healthcare services, and guiding policy decisions aimed at reducing transmission through targeted interventions like pre-exposure prophylaxis or improved linkages to treatment.
